positional.title: Positional Data
positional.note: Positional data requires Skyfield computation on the server.

planets.title: Tonight's Planet Outlook
planets.footNote: Times shown in local station time. Visibility varies based on seeing conditions and sky darkness.

meteorShowers.title: Meteor Showers
meteorShowers.subtitle: Upcoming meteor showers visible from your location over the next year.
meteorShowers.empty: No meteor showers in the next year.
meteorShowers.footNote: Rates shown are predicted zenith hourly rate (ZHR) under ideal conditions.

solarEclipses.title: Solar Eclipses
solarEclipses.subtitle: The next solar eclipses visible from your location.
solarEclipses.noEclipses: No solar eclipses visible from your location in the next several years.
solarEclipses.footNote: Visibility can vary based on cloud cover and your exact location.
solarEclipses.descTotal: Total solar eclipse. The Sun will be completely covered by the Moon.
solarEclipses.descAnnular: The Moon will pass in front of the Sun, leaving a brilliant ring of sunlight visible.
solarEclipses.descPartial: The Moon will pass in front of the Sun, covering a portion of its disk.

lunarEclipses.title: Lunar Eclipses
lunarEclipses.subtitle: The next lunar eclipses visible from your location.
lunarEclipses.noEclipses: No lunar eclipses visible from your location in the next several years.
lunarEclipses.footNote: Eclipse visibility can vary based on weather and your exact location.
lunarEclipses.descTotal: Total lunar eclipse. The entire Moon will pass through Earth's umbra.
lunarEclipses.descPartial: Partial lunar eclipse. Part of the Moon will pass through Earth's umbra.
lunarEclipses.descPenumbral: Penumbral lunar eclipse. A subtle dimming as the Moon passes through Earth's penumbra.
